exampleTranslation: My nephew is three years old.
I need to go to the pharmacy.

exampleSentence: Vou ao correio enviar uma carta.
exampleTranslation: I'm going to the post office to send a letter.
Shall we go to the movies today?
I live on the third floor.

exampleSentence: Um copo de vinho, por favor.
notes: Vinho tinto = red wine; vinho branco = white wine.

exampleSentence: O bolo de chocolate é ótimo.

exampleTranslation: I want a strawberry ice cream.

exampleSentence: O céu está cheio de nuvens.
exampleTranslation: The sky is full of clouds.
I need to wash the clothes.
I'm going to clean the house.
I wake up at six o'clock.
I don't want to miss the bus.
notes: First person present: perco. Also means 'to miss' (bus, opportunity).
Salgado = also a popular savory Brazilian snack.
Gostoso is very common in Brazil.
Response: São duas horas = It's two o'clock.

exampleTranslation: I don't know where it is.

notes: In Minas Gerais, trem can mean 'thing'.

exampleTranslation: The airplane is going to take off.

notes: Dor de cabeça = headache; dor de barriga = stomachache.

exampleSentence: O futebol é o esporte mais popular.
exampleTranslation: Soccer is the most popular sport.

notes: False friend: time in PT = team, not time.

exampleSentence: Eu leio o jornal toda manhã.
exampleTranslation: I read the newspaper every morning.
